title: Frequently The Woods Are Pink,
content:
Frequently the woods are pink,
    Frequently are brown;
    Frequently the hills undress
    Behind my native town.

    Oft a head is crested
    I was wont to see,
    And as oft a cranny
    Where it used to be.

    And the earth, they tell me,
    On its axis turned, --
    Wonderful rotation
    By but twelve performed!
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Frequently The Woods Are Pink,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
